summary: Microsoft documents the Chat icon through the device policy ConfigureChatIcon under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat. The app implementation was aligned to that official policy surface on 2026-03-13 and now uses ChatIcon=2, the documented Hide state that removes the Chat icon from the taskbar by default while still allowing users to show or hide it in Settings.

setting.name: Taskbar Chat Icon
setting.casual_explanation: Windows 11 can show a Chat icon on the taskbar. People often hide it when they do not use Chat or want a cleaner taskbar.
setting.why_people_change_it: People hide the Chat icon to reduce taskbar clutter and remove a feature they do not use.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Hiding the Chat icon makes the taskbar cleaner, but it also removes a quick entry point to Chat. Using the Hide state is less invasive than the Disabled state because users can still show or hide the icon in Settings.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft documents ConfigureChatIcon in the Experience Policy CSP and Taskbar.admx. The official control is a device policy with states Not configured, Show, Hide, and Disabled. The app previously wrote HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\TaskbarMn = 0, but that implementation mismatch was corrected on 2026-03-13 to use the documented machine-policy path HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat\ChatIcon = 2 (Hide). The app intentionally uses Hide rather than Disabled so the icon is hidden by default without locking users out of changing it in Settings.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The Chat icon follows the defaults for the Windows edition.

allowed_values.label: Show
allowed_values.meaning: The Chat icon is shown on the taskbar by default, and users can still show or hide it in Settings.

allowed_values.label: Hide
allowed_values.meaning: The Chat icon is hidden by default, and users can still show or hide it in Settings. This is the state the app now applies.

allowed_values.label: Disabled
allowed_values.meaning: The Chat icon is not displayed and users cannot show or hide it in Settings.

targets.notes: Windows default is missing (key absent). The app intentionally uses Hide (2) rather than Disabled (3) to match the lighter-weight current behavior of removing the icon without hard-locking the Settings toggle.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app now writes HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat\ChatIcon = 2, which matches the documented ConfigureChatIcon policy and the Hide state validated in this record. The previous HKCU TaskbarMn write was removed as part of the implementation-mismatch fix on 2026-03-13.

windows_defaults.label: Windows Chat policy default

states.rationale: Leave the official device policy unset.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents the policy default as not configured.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want the edition default for Chat
avoid_for: Users who want the Chat icon hidden by policy
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Chat to follow the Windows edition default.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want an explicit hide policy for the icon.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its default Chat icon behavior.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide Chat icon
intended_for: Users who want a cleaner taskbar without removing Settings control
avoid_for: People who want Chat shown by default
Admins who want the stronger Disabled state instead of Hide

states.rationale: Use the documented Hide state in ConfigureChatIcon.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the Chat icon hidden by default but still want users to be able to change it in Settings.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a hard-disable policy that prevents users from changing the icon visibility.
tradeoffs: The Chat icon is hidden by default.
Users can still show or hide it in Settings because this uses Hide, not Disabled.
recommended_profiles.notes: apply_allowed=true. The proof chain is explicit: Microsoft documents ConfigureChatIcon and local Taskbar.admx defines value 2 as Hide. The app now writes the same machine-policy path and value.

evidence.title: Microsoft Policy CSP: Experience / ConfigureChatIcon
evidence.summary: Official Microsoft documentation for the Chat icon policy and its allowed values 0 through 3.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Taskbar.admx ConfigureChatIcon mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file maps ConfigureChatIcon to Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat and defines enum values 1 Show, 2 Hide, and 3 Disabled.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Taskbar.adml ConfigureChatIcon help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains the Show, Hide, and Disabled Chat icon policy states and the user experience for each.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat\ChatIcon = 2 for the current tweak.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact ConfigureChatIcon device-policy surface, enum values, and Hide behavior. The app now writes the same machine-policy path and uses the documented Hide value 2, so the implementation matches the researched control surface. recommended_for_general_users remains false because the practical benefit is mostly cosmetic and Windows defaults may already suit most users.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="ConfigureChatIcon" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Windows Chat"> ... <enum id="ConfigureChatIcon_Action" valueName="ChatIcon" ...> ... <decimal value="1" /> ... <decimal value="2" /> ... <decimal value="3" />; Taskbar.adml: If you enable this policy setting and set it to Hide, the Chat icon will be hidden by default. Users can show or hide it in Settings.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact device-policy key and ChatIcon enum surface; local ADML confirms the Hide behavior. The app now writes the documented machine-policy path with value 2.
